header.title: HERO: The System Is Operationally Functional, But Experientially Invisible.

content: We mapped three complete patient journeys at PGIMER — a new patient, an existing offline patient, and an online pre-registered patient. The findings are consistent across all three: the system tracks cards and CR numbers, but it does not track patients. The moment the card leaves the patient's hand, they become invisible.
Based on ground research at PGIMER Cardiology OPD, March 2026

header.title: PROCESS: Steps 1–3: Before the Patient Sits Down

steps.heading: Register & Choose Slot
text: Via app, WhatsApp bot, PGIMER portal, or IVR helpline (for non-smartphone users). Patient selects department + preferred date/time window. System auto-stamps OPD card digitally. Confirmation + QR code sent via all active channels.

details.title: Backend
details.text: Queue engine assigns position. OPD card auto-generated. QR code created. Slot confirmed.

details.title: Cost Components
details.text: WhatsApp Business API: ₹xxx/msg. SMS Gateway: ₹xxx/msg. IVR: ₹xxx/call. Portal dev: one-time.

steps.heading: Pre-Visit Reminder (24hrs Before)
text: Automated reminder: date, time slot, department, floor/room, documents to carry. Includes map link for first-timers. For non-smartphone: AI voice call in Hindi/Punjabi. Retry 12hrs before if missed.

details.title: Backend
details.text: Auto-triggered 24hrs before slot. AI call system (Hindi, Punjabi, English). Retry logic on failure.

details.title: Impact
details.text: Reduces no-shows by 30-40% (evidence-based). Prepares patient mentally. Reduces Day-0 confusion.

steps.heading: Frictionless Check-In at Hospital
text: Online patients: Scan QR at department kiosk or tap "I've arrived" in app. Zero queues. Walk-in patients: Self-service kiosk at entry → Enter Aadhaar/phone → System creates record → Prints token + QR → Added to queue instantly. Assisted counter: For elderly/non-digital patients — volunteer registers them, explains process, prints token.

details.title: Backend
details.text: Real-time queue position assigned. Department dashboard updated. Wait time calculated from avg consult duration.

details.title: Cost Components
details.text: Kiosk hardware: ₹xxx each. 10 kiosks for Phase 1 departments. Token printer: ₹xxx each.

header.title: BEFOREAFTER: How This Changes Life for Doctors & Support Staff
header.subtitle: A system that only serves patients but burdens staff will not be adopted. This reduces friction for everyone.

before.heading: Doctor — Today
before.text: Constant door interruptions. Patients asking "When is my turn?" every 2 minutes. No patient context before walk-in. Paper files missing. Mental overhead of managing anxious crowd outside. Stress from confrontation.

after.heading: Doctor — With System
after.text: Zero door interruptions. One-click "Next Patient" button. Patient summary pre-loaded (Phase 2). No crowd management. Doctor focuses entirely on the patient in front of them. Reduced confrontation = reduced burnout.

before.heading: Support Staff — Today
before.text: Manually managing queues. Answering "When is my turn?" hundreds of times daily. Handling frustration and occasional aggression. Manual card stamping with breakable terminals. No fallback when hardware fails.

after.heading: Support Staff — With System
after.text: Queue is self-managed. Screen and notifications answer the #1 question automatically. Reduced confrontations. Digital stamping eliminates terminal dependency. Staff focuses on patients who genuinely need help.

before.heading: Administration — Today
before.text: No real-time visibility into patient load. No data on wait times. No feedback mechanism. No bottleneck identification. Infrastructure failures discovered when queues explode.

after.heading: Administration — With System
after.text: Live dashboard: patient flow across departments. Historical analytics for planning. Patient feedback scores. Proactive infrastructure alerts. Evidence for budget requests and accreditation.

header.title: GRIDCARDS: Digital Patient Platform & App
header.subtitle: Once Phase 1 is stable and proven, layer on a comprehensive patient app — a digital companion for every visit and between visits. This is the long-term relationship with the patient.

cards.heading: 📋 Visit History
text: Complete timeline of every PGI visit — date, department, doctor, diagnosis, prescriptions. Replaces the fat paper file.

cards.heading: 🔬 Test Results
text: Auto-uploaded from PGI labs. Photo upload for outside lab results. All linked to visit timeline.

cards.heading: 👨‍⚕️ Doctor's Notes
text: Key points from each consultation in simple language. Diagnosis, observations, recommendations. Patient can revisit anytime.

cards.heading: 💊 Medication Tracker
text: Current prescriptions, dosage, timing. Reminders. Refill alerts. Drug interaction warnings.

cards.heading: 🤖 AI Health Assistant
text: Ask questions in Hindi/English between visits. "When should I take this?" "What does this report mean?" Bridge between consultations.

cards.heading: 👪 Family Access
text: Add family members as caregivers. Son in Gurugram tracks father's appointments, reports, medications remotely. Shared health dashboard.

cards.heading: 📊 Health Trends
text: Visualise BP, sugar, cholesterol over time. Simple graphs. Visible to patient and doctor both.

cards.heading: 📅 Appointments
text: Book, reschedule, cancel. View slots by department/doctor. Auto-reminders at 48hrs, 24hrs, 2hrs.

cards.heading: 🏥 PGI Navigator
text: Indoor wayfinding. "How to get to Cardiology from main gate?" Step-by-step with landmarks. Critical for first-timers and elderly.

header.title: TIMELINE: Implementation Roadmap
header.subtitle: Phased rollout — prove it in one department, then scale. Each phase has clear deliverables, costs, and success metrics.

timeline.heading: Phase 1A: Pilot in Cardiology OPD
timeline.text: Build: Queue management engine. WhatsApp/SMS notification system. 3 department display screens. 2 self-service kiosks. AI calling integration. Assisted registration desk setup. Deliver: Live queue for Cardiology OPD. All 5 notification channels active. Test loop priority re-entry system. Post-visit feedback mechanism. Measure: Patient satisfaction score. Average perceived wait time. Door-crowding reduction. Staff feedback.

timeline.heading: Phase 1B: Expand to 4 More Departments
timeline.text: Build: Scale queue system to Neurology, Gastroenterology, Orthopaedics, Ophthalmology. 8 additional screens. 4 additional kiosks. Staff training across departments. Deliver: 5 departments fully live. Cross-department dashboard for admin. Feedback data loop operational. Measure: Satisfaction trend across departments. No-show rate change. Queue depth patterns.

timeline.heading: Phase 1C: Full OPD Coverage
timeline.text: Build: All remaining OPD departments. Pharmacy queue integration. Lab queue integration. Audio announcement system. Full kiosk network. Deliver: Every OPD department has live queue, screens, and notifications. End-to-end patient journey tracked. Measure: Institution-wide satisfaction. Average wait reduction. Staff time reclaimed.

timeline.heading: Phase 2: Digital Patient Platform & App
timeline.text: Build: PGIMER patient app (iOS + Android). Visit history + doctor notes. Test result upload/integration. Medication tracker. AI health assistant. Family access dashboard. Health trends. Indoor wayfinding. Deliver: Full patient companion app. Digital health records for every registered patient. AI assistant in Hindi + English. Measure: App adoption rate. Medication adherence improvement. Follow-up compliance. Patient NPS.

timeline.heading: Phase 3: Intelligence & Scale
timeline.text: Build: AI-powered wait time predictions. Research data pipeline. No-show prediction model. Cross-department referral system. Model documentation for PGI Satellite Sangrur. Deliver: Predictive operations dashboard. Research-grade anonymised datasets. Replicable model for other centres. Measure: Prediction accuracy. Research papers enabled. Scalability validation.

header.title: TABLELAYOUT: Execution Cost Summary
header.subtitle: Estimated costs for each phase. All figures are indicative and will be refined in the detailed technical proposal post-meeting.

rows: Queue engine, 5-channel notifications, 3 screens, 2 kiosks, test loop fix, feedback
Scale to 5 total departments, 8 screens, 4 kiosks, admin dashboard
All OPD departments, pharmacy + lab integration, audio system, full kiosk network
iOS + Android app, health records, AI assistant, medication tracker, family access
AI predictions, research data, cross-department referrals, Sangrur replication model
Over 18 months. Phase 1A can demonstrate impact within 90 days.
